topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The late-night silence of the dorm room was usually a comfort, but tonight, it felt charged, thick with unspoken tension. I was meticulously organizing my textbooks, a task I usually found soothing, but now my fingers felt clumsy, my movements jerky. When I finally glanced over, I saw him. Liam, my roommate, was perched on the edge of his bed, his attention entirely fixed on my desk. His eyes weren't on my notes; they were tracing the lines of my open journal, his brow furrowed in a way that felt accusatory. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, a sensation that made my breath catch. I could feel the sudden, intense urge to snatch the book away, to hide the contents before he could see another word. My jaw tightened, and I forced a small, brittle smile, hoping he wouldn't notice the tremor running through my hands.

[story 2]

It started subtly—a misplaced bookmark, a slightly askew page. But then, one Tuesday afternoon, I walked in to find my private diary splayed open on Liam’s desk. The ink was dark, detailing my most vulnerable anxieties, my secret aspirations. A flush crept up my neck, hot and undeniable. I stood there for a long moment, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. The air in the room seemed to thin. When Liam looked up, his expression was unreadable, a strange mixture of curiosity and something sharper. I mumbled an excuse, my voice sounding thin and reedy, while my eyes darted around the room, searching for an escape route. I felt a desperate need to either confess everything or somehow erase the evidence before he could use it to manipulate me.

[story 3]

I couldn't shake the feeling of being watched. Every time I turned my back, I felt a prickling awareness, a sense that something was lingering in the periphery of my vision. I finally cornered him near the communal kitchen, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead amplifying the frantic rhythm in my chest. "What are you doing with my things?" I managed, my voice pitched higher than I intended. He looked up slowly, a faint, almost imperceptible smirk playing on his lips. He didn't deny it. Instead, he leaned back against the counter, crossing his arms, his posture radiating a quiet confidence that made my skin crawl. The sheer audacity of the situation made my throat constrict. I wanted to shout, to demand the book back, but all that escaped was a shaky exhale, a surrender to the uncomfortable reality that he had seen more than he should have.
